cost_model_ops._note: v5: add=2n for ALL real x,y (ADD-T1). add_pos and add_gen distinction eliminated.

note_DEML: DEML(x,1) = exp(-x) costs 1n (single operator), cheaper than explicit neg(2n)+exp(1n)=3n

equations.name: Nernst Equation

cost_breakdown.note: subtract — sign is fixed (sub, not add_gen)

equations.sign_note: Uses sub(2n) not add_gen(11n) because the correction term is subtracted from E_std by definition
cross_domain: Reaction free energy ΔG = ΔG° + RT·ln(Q) is the same equation via ΔG = -nFE
Nernst is 11n; ΔG form is 16n — difference is sub(2n) vs add_gen(11n)

equations.name: Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation

cost_breakdown.note: convert to log10; ln10 is a compile-time constant
v5 (ADD-T1): add=2n for all real; log10 term can be negative — no longer a cost penalty

equations.v5_note: updated: add_gen 11n→2n saves 9n (ADD-T1). General and near-pKa costs now identical: both 7n. The positive/general distinction is eliminated.
equations.sign_note: log10([A-]/[HA]) < 0 when [A-] < [HA] — sign ambiguity no longer affects cost in v5
cross_domain: pH definition: pH = -log10([H+]) = 5n
Henderson-Hasselbalch extends pH with a signed correction — hence 16n vs 5n

equations.name: Chemical Equilibrium Constant

cost_breakdown.note: product C raised to stoichiometric coefficient
product D raised to stoichiometric coefficient
reactant A raised to stoichiometric coefficient
reactant B raised to stoichiometric coefficient

equations.scaling_note: Cost scales as 3p*pow + (p-1)*mul per side for p species; general formula: 8*(p+q)/2 + 2 for p products, q reactants
cross_domain: Reaction quotient Q in Nernst/ΔG has the same structure
Activity product in solubility Ksp is a simpler special case (integer exponents only)

equations.name: Rate Law

cost_breakdown.note: concentration A to reaction order m
concentration B to reaction order n

cross_domain: Same structure as power-law scaling in physics and economics
For integer orders (m,n ∈ {1,2}): pow(x,1)=0n (identity), pow(x,2)=mul(x,x)=2n — simplifies significantly

equations.name: Michaelis-Menten Kinetics

cost_breakdown.note: both Km and [S] are positive concentrations

cross_domain: Structurally identical to Langmuir adsorption isotherm: θ = K[A]/(1 + K[A])
Same as Hill equation with n=1
Same cost (7n) as Arrhenius equation k = A*exp(-Ea/RT)
Universal saturating-response template: 7n

equations.name: Beer-Lambert Law
cross_domain: Transmittance form (5n) is isomorphic to: radioactive decay [A](t)=A0*exp(-kt), RC discharge, first-order reaction, optical depth
Absorbance = -log(T) adds ln(T)=1n + neg=2n = 3n on top of transmittance = 8n total for A from I0,I

equations.name: pH Definition

cost_breakdown.note: natural log of hydrogen ion concentration
convert to base-10 log; ln10 is constant

cross_domain: Decibel: dB = 10*log10(P1/P2) — same log structure, 7n with mul
Richter scale: M = log10(A/A0) — same 5n structure
SNR in dB: 10*log10(signal/noise) — 7n
All log-ratio scales form the 5-7n family

equations.name: Gibbs-Helmholtz Equation
cross_domain: Used to extract ΔH from temperature-dependent equilibrium data (van't Hoff plots)
van't Hoff: d(ln K)/d(1/T) = -ΔH/R — same 7n structure plus ln(K)=1n = 8n total

equations.name: Reaction Free Energy

cost_breakdown.note: v5 (ADD-T1): add=2n for all real; RT*ln(Q) sign is no longer a cost factor

equations.v5_note: updated: add_gen 11n→2n saves 9n (ADD-T1). breakdown: mul(R,T)=2n + ln(Q)=1n + mul(RT,lnQ)=2n + add=2n = 7n
equations.sign_note: RT*ln(Q) > 0 when Q > 1 (forward unfavorable), < 0 when Q < 1 (forward favorable) — sign ambiguity no longer affects cost in v5
cross_domain: Nernst equation E = E° - RT/(nF)*ln(Q) is the same physics via ΔG = -nFE
v5: Nernst costs 11n (sub=2n + extra div+mul terms), ΔG form costs 7n (add=2n). ΔG form is now CHEAPER by 4n.
v4: Nernst 11n vs ΔG 16n (ΔG was more expensive due to add_gen=11n)
Lesson (v5): with add=2n = sub=2n, the key cost driver is structure (muls and divs), not sign convention

equations.name: Integrated First-Order Rate Law

cost_breakdown.note: exp(-kt) via single DEML operator — cheaper than neg(2n)+exp(1n)=3n

cross_domain: EXACT isomorphism (all 5n): radioactive decay N(t)=N0*exp(-λt), RC discharge V(t)=V0*exp(-t/RC), Beer-Lambert transmittance, population decay, optical attenuation
Universal exponential decay template: 5n
Half-life t_1/2 = ln(2)/k adds ln(2)=const, div=2n → 2n extra if needed

cost_summary.key_cross_domain: = Reaction ΔG = -nFE (different form, 7n in v5; Nernst now MORE expensive by 4n due to extra div+mul)
v5: both general and near-pKa = 7n (add=2n, ADD-T1); v4: 16n general, 8n near-pKa
Same structure as reaction quotient Q in Nernst/ΔG
Transmittance form (5n) = first-order decay = RC discharge
v5: 7n (add=2n, ADD-T1, saves 9n); Nernst (11n) now MORE expensive by 4n
Universal 5n decay: radioactive, RC, optical, biological

key_observations.title: Nernst vs ΔG form cost gap (v5 reversal)
key_observations.detail: v4: Nernst E = E° - RT/(nF)*ln(Q) costs 11n; ΔG = ΔG° + RT*ln(Q) costs 16n. Same physics (ΔG=-nFE), 5n gap. v5 (ADD-T1): add=2n = sub=2n, so sign convention no longer drives cost. ΔG form drops to 7n. Nernst stays at 11n (it has an extra div(nF)+mul chain). The gap reverses: ΔG form is now cheaper by 4n.

key_observations.title: Universal 5n exponential decay
key_observations.detail: First-order decay, radioactive decay, RC discharge, Beer-Lambert transmittance, population decay — all share the template x0 * DEML(k*t, 1). Exact structural isomorphism at 5n.

key_observations.title: 7n saturating-response template
key_observations.detail: Michaelis-Menten, Langmuir adsorption, Hill (n=1), Arrhenius all cost 7n. add(2n) + div(2n) + mul(2n) + ln(1n) = 7n is the universal saturating-curve skeleton. (v5: add_pos=3n replaced by add=2n, but Michaelis-Menten's add(Km,[S]) saves only 1n; 7n total unchanged since MM uses add_pos path.)

key_observations.title: DEML saves 2n vs naive exp(-x)
key_observations.detail: Naive: neg(x)=2n + exp(result)=1n = 3n. DEML(x,1)=1n. Savings = 2n per exponential decay term.

key_observations.title: add_gen obsolete in v5 (ADD-T1)
key_observations.detail: v4: add_gen=11n dominated signed sums. Henderson-Hasselbalch general and ΔG both paid 11n for one signed addition. v5: add=2n for ALL real x,y. Both equations save 9n. Sign-definiteness is no longer the primary cost driver. The key bottleneck shifts entirely to structural complexity (mul/div chains, not addition sign).
